mission.title: Install and Configure Kong Gateway
mission.description: Kong Gateway is a cloud-native, scalable API gateway designed for high performance and extensibility through plugins. It is ideal for managing microservices and API traffic, providing features like load balancing, authentication, and advanced AI traffic capabilities. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management versus the powerful features it offers.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Kong CLI tool to manage your Kong Gateway installation.

```bash

brew install konghq/tap/kong

choco install kong

```

> Use Homebrew on macOS for installation.
> Use Chocolatey on Windows for installation.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kong-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kong-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Install Kong Gateway
steps.description:
Install Kong Gateway using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm repo add kong https://charts.konghq.com

helm repo update

kubectl create namespace kong

helm install kong kong/kong --namespace kong --version 3.9.1

```

> Add the Kong Helm repository.
> Update your Helm repositories.
> Create a namespace for Kong.
> Install Kong Gateway in the 'kong' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your Kong Gateway.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kong/kong/main/kubernetes/kong-ingress.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kong/kong/main/kubernetes/kong-monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply the ingress configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kong Gateway
uninstall.description:
This will remove Kong Gateway and all associated resources. Be aware of potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kong --namespace kong

kubectl delete namespace kong

```

> Uninstall Kong from the 'kong' namespace.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kong Gateway
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get kong -n kong -o yaml > kong-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kong kong/kong --namespace kong --version 3.9.1

kubectl get pods -n kong

```

> Backup current Kong configuration.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kong -n kong -o yaml > kong-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kong ingress controller not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kong ingress controller has not been installed.

**Fix:**
Ensure you have installed Kong using the Helm command provided.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Database connection settings are incorrect or the database is down.

**Fix:**
Check your database configuration in the values.yaml file and ensure the database is running.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Configuration errors or missing environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kong' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Ingress not routing traffic
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Ingress rules may not be correctly configured.

**Fix:**
Verify your ingress resource definitions and ensure they are applied correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new plugins, improved performance of existing plugins, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review the new plugin documentation and update configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get services -n kong' showing the Kong Gateway services running with external IPs assigned.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
